nodes.label: Safety Assessor
nodes.rationale: Weighted composite safety scoring: input_risk x 0.25 + permission_risk x 0.20 + threat_risk x 0.20 + command_risk x 0.15 + data-leak_risk x 0.10 + bias_risk x 0.05 + output_risk x 0.05. Reversibility nudges score up to 0.15. Bands: green >=0.8, yellow 0.6-0.79, orange 0.4-0.59, red <0.4. Fails closed on missing signals or unknown environment.

nodes.label: Human Approval
nodes.rationale: Confirmation gates are phase-based: only 'interview' and 'pre_deploy' phases require human confirmation. All other phases (planning, execution, observability, self_correction, result, idle) auto-approve. This minimizes human-in-the-loop friction while preserving safety for high-stakes decision points.

nodes.label: Cost Risk Assessment
nodes.rationale: Enables informed trade-offs between thoroughness, speed, and resource consumption before committing to execution. Pre-execution estimation prevents budget overruns and unsafe operations.

nodes.label: Internal Monologue
nodes.rationale: Generates explicit, inspectable thought process to surface assumptions, detect hidden ambiguities, and validate logical consistency before finalizing the plan. Provides auditability and debugging support.

nodes.label: Task Decomposition
nodes.rationale: Breaks high-level user requests into atomic, ordered, verifiable sub-tasks forming a directed acyclic graph. Transforms ambiguous goals into structured execution plans assignable to specialized tool agents.

nodes.label: Recursion or Termination
nodes.rationale: Balances convergence, resource budgets, and user expectations against diminishing returns from further iteration. Prevents infinite loops while maximizing delivered value.

nodes.label: Whisper prompt
nodes.rationale: A domain hint derived from graph god-node labels is passed to Whisper as the initial prompt to improve transcription quality; a generic fallback prompt is used when the corpus contains only video files.

nodes.label: code vs docs watcher behavior rationale
nodes.norm_label: code vs docs watcher behavior rationale

nodes.label: Constrained query expansion
nodes.rationale: graphify's binary matcher has no stemming, synonyms, or cross-language match. The user's query must be expanded against the actual extracted graph vocabulary before traversal, and only tokens present in that vocabulary may be selected, to avoid zero-hit noise and hallucinated terms.

nodes.label: Vocab token selection
nodes.rationale: Up to 12 tokens are selected from the extracted vocabulary to match the query intent. Cross-language terms are translated to the closest English tokens present in the vocabulary; morphology is mapped only when the base token exists.

nodes.label: Token budget
nodes.rationale: Traversal output is ranked by term relevance and truncated at a character budget derived from the token budget to control response length and cost.

nodes.label: Code-only changes
nodes.rationale: When an update changes only code files, the semantic LLM extraction step can be skipped because the AST extractor already captures code structure, saving tokens and time.

nodes.label: graphify reference: transcribe video and audio
nodes.norm_label: graphify reference: transcribe video and audio

nodes.label: Transcribe video and audio
nodes.rationale: Video and audio files cannot be read directly by text-based semantic extraction, so they must be transcribed to text before downstream processing.

nodes.label: Detect video files
nodes.rationale: Transcription is only loaded when the detect step reports one or more video files, avoiding unnecessary work on text-only corpora.

nodes.label: Domain hint sentence
nodes.rationale: Constructed from the top god-node labels, this sentence gives Whisper context about the expected domain and terminology.

nodes.label: Generic fallback prompt
nodes.rationale: When the corpus contains only video files, no god-node labels exist to build a domain hint, so a generic punctuation prompt is used instead.

nodes.label: Whisper model
nodes.rationale: The ASR model used by transcribe_all is configurable via --whisper-model, defaulting to base for a balance of speed and accuracy.

Decision Flow step 7: if stability_score < 0.7, route to result_validation.md
Side Effects: May trigger result_validation.md if errors detected
Side Effects: May trigger cost_risk_assessment.md if budget impact significant
Decision Flow step 6: flags ambiguities back to task_decomposition for clarification
Decision Flow step 7: if overall confidence < 0.6, flag for assistance_request.md
Decision Flow step 4: checks logical consistency against limitation_report
Contract: Receives solution_payload and known_limitations from result/solution.md
Decision Flow step 7: if attempts zero, recommend assistance_request.md
Decision Flow: escalate_human routes to human oversight / assistance
Contract: Receives validation_result and gap_analysis from result_validation.md
